summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has closed the Strait of Hormuz to vessel traffic, citing Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments. Israel's military confirms striking Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon in response to projectile launches, while stating commitment to the ceasefire. New U.S. diplomatic envoy Vance will travel to Switzerland for talks with Iran, with the Lebanon ceasefire and nuclear file on the agenda.
key_points: IRGC announced closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, calling it the first step in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments. (Source: @yediotnews25/131681)
IDF confirms launching airstrikes in southern Lebanon overnight, saying they responded to approximately 50 projectiles launched by Hezbollah at Israeli forces. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)
Iran's Foreign Ministry stated Iran fulfilled its obligations and the other side must force the Israeli entity to stop its attacks on Lebanon. (Source: @yediotnews25/131684)
Iran's negotiating delegation, led by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf with Abbas Araghchi, has departed for Switzerland for technical talks with the United States. (Source: @VahidOnline/76551)
New U.S. diplomatic envoy Vance said the Lebanon ceasefire file is the main topic to be discussed with Iran in Switzerland, alongside the nuclear file. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372009)
Iranian media reported Iranian missiles may be launched toward Israel tonight if aggression against Lebanon continues. (Source: @almogboker78/17531)
Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Turkey's foreign ministers and Trump's senior adviser held a meeting on Iran, emphasizing a Tehran-Washington memorandum of understanding. (Source: @SharghDaily/167132)
Two Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon identified: Sergeant First Class Yoav Klein and Staff Sergeant Nir Ben Ari. (Source: @amitsegal/62375)

sections.executive_summary: Iran's IRGC closed the Strait of Hormuz to vessel traffic, citing Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments. The IDF struck dozens of Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon overnight, responding to approximately 50 projectiles launched at Israeli forces. New U.S. diplomatic envoy Vance and Iran's negotiating delegation are heading to Switzerland for talks focused on the Lebanon ceasefire and nuclear file.

points: The IRGC's Khatam terror HQ announced the Strait of Hormuz is now closed to vessels, citing Israeli crimes in Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments. The statement emphasized this is the first step, with further measures planned if aggression continues. (Source: @shin_persian/7141)

Iran's Foreign Ministry stated Iran has stood by its obligations and demanded the Israeli entity stop its attacks on Lebanon, warning that if the other side refuses to fulfill commitments, all understandings will be in danger. (Source: @yediotnews25/131684)

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a full statement announcing closure of the Strait of Hormuz to vessel traffic and maritime shipping lanes, calling it the first step in response to the enemy's violations. (Source: @yediotnews25/131681)

Iran's naval force issued a statement confirming the Strait of Hormuz is closed to all ships due to Israeli crimes in Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire implementation, warning vessels not to approach. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79400)

A senior Iranian source told Reuters the IRGC are the real winners of the war, positioned to benefit from sanctions removal after decades of operating sanctions-evasion mechanisms. (Source: @asafroz/50527)

Iran's negotiating delegation, led by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf with Abbas Araghchi, departed for Switzerland; the delegation includes Abdolnasser Hemmati, Ali Bagheri Kani, Kazem Gharibabadi, Ismail Baqaei, and Hamid Bordan. (Source: @VahidOnline/76551)
Iranian media reported Iranian missiles may be launched toward Israel tonight if aggression against Lebanon continues. (Source: @almogboker78/17531)

The IDF confirms launching airstrikes in southern Lebanon overnight and this morning in response to repeated ceasefire violations by Hezbollah, which launched approximately 50 projectiles at Israeli forces. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)

IDF struck dozens of Hezbollah sites and operatives across southern Lebanon, including rocket launchers, weapon depots, and command centers, in response to what it called blatant violations. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)
Senior Israeli political sources stated the IDF attacked 300 terror targets and eliminated approximately 100 operatives in response to Hezbollah attacks. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)

Two Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon were identified: Sergeant First Class Yoav Klein, 21, from Herzliya, a tank crewman in Battalion 52, 401st Brigade; and Staff Sergeant Nir Ben Ari, 21, from Kerem Maharal, in the Maglan unit, Commando Brigade. (Source: @amitsegal/62375)

The IDF stated it is committed to the ceasefire agreement per political echelon directives while continuing to act to remove threats to Israel and IDF troops from south of the yellow line. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)
An Israeli source was cited as saying Iranian missiles may be launched toward Israel tonight if aggression against Lebanon continues. (Source: @almogboker78/17531)
The ceasefire in Lebanon was violated after Israel and Hezbollah had announced a ceasefire to take effect on 19.06 at 16:00, with attacks resuming afterward. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)
No specific operational developments regarding Gaza or the West Bank were reported in the provided messages.

Hezbollah launched approximately 50 projectiles at Israeli forces operating in southern Lebanon overnight, which the IDF described as repeated violations of the ceasefire agreement. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)
Five people were reported killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon this morning according to initial reports. (Source: @asafroz/50531)
Israeli military sources stated the IDF attacked 300 terror targets and eliminated approximately 100 operatives in southern Lebanon in response to Hezbollah attacks. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)
The ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was set to take effect on 19.06 at 16:00 but was violated overnight with exchanges of fire. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)
Families of the dead are monitoring news and awaiting Hezbollah's permission to recover bodies. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372026)
The Israeli military has not commented on claims that it attempted to advance toward the Ali Taher ridge near Nabatieh overnight. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)
Artillery shelling was reported in Nabatieh al-Fawqa according to local monitoring channels. (Source: @redlinkleb/27376)
No specific operational developments regarding Syria were reported in the provided messages.

The IRGC announced closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, a critical chokepoint for Gulf state maritime traffic, citing Israeli attacks in Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments. (Source: @shin_persian/7141)

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a full statement declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed to vessel traffic and maritime shipping lanes, warning vessels not to approach. (Source: @naya_foriraq/79400)
Iranian media reported Iranian missiles may be launched toward Israel tonight if aggression against Lebanon continues, which could threaten Gulf state security. (Source: @almogboker78/17531)

Trump stated that no tolls will be imposed in the Strait of Hormuz except in America's favor if the agreement is not completed, citing services as guardian angel for Middle Eastern countries. (Source: @VahidOnline/76555)

key_developments: IRGC closed the Strait of Hormuz to all vessel traffic, calling it the first step in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon and U.S. violations of ceasefire commitments, with further measures threatened. (Source: @shin_persian/7141)

IDF struck dozens of Hezbollah sites across southern Lebanon including rocket launchers, weapon depots, and command centers after Hezbollah launched approximately 50 projectiles at Israeli forces overnight. (Source: @shin_persian/7140)
Senior Israeli sources stated the IDF attacked 300 terror targets and eliminated approximately 100 operatives in southern Lebanon in response to Hezbollah attacks. (Source: @yediotnews25/131691)
Iran's negotiating delegation led by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf with Abbas Araghchi departed for Switzerland for technical talks with the United States delegation. (Source: @VahidOnline/76551)
New U.S. diplomatic envoy Vance confirmed the Lebanon ceasefire file is the main topic to be discussed with Iran in Switzerland alongside the nuclear file. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372009)

Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Turkey's foreign ministers and Trump's senior adviser held a meeting emphasizing the importance of a Tehran-Washington memorandum of understanding for regional de-escalation. (Source: @SharghDaily/167132)
Two Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon identified: Yoav Klein and Nir Ben Ari, both 21, from the 401st Brigade and Commando Brigade respectively. (Source: @amitsegal/62375)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment has escalated significantly with the IRGC's closure of the Strait of Hormuz representing a major strategic development affecting global maritime traffic. Iran has explicitly threatened further escalation, including potential missile launches toward Israel, if Lebanese operations continue. The IRGC's statement that this is only the first step signals potential for additional asymmetric measures. Meanwhile, active combat continues in southern Lebanon with the ceasefire effectively collapsed, as evidenced by Hezbollah's 50-projectile barrage and Israel's reported strikes on 300 targets. The convergence of these theaters creates a multi-front escalation risk.
sections.regional_response: Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Turkey's foreign ministers, along with Trump's senior adviser, convened to discuss Iran and regional developments, emphasizing the importance of a Tehran-Washington memorandum of understanding for de-escalation. Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Army Chief Asim Munir are traveling to Switzerland to participate in technical negotiations between Tehran and Washington. Iran's negotiating delegation, led by parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf with senior officials from the central bank, national security council, and foreign ministry, departed for Zurich. The diplomatic track appears active even as military escalation continues on the ground.
sections.intelligence_notes: The IRGC's closure of the Strait of Hormuz appears calibrated as a coercive measure short of direct military engagement, designed to pressure the U.S. and Israel while maintaining plausible deniability. The IRGC source's statement to Reuters about being positioned to benefit from sanctions removal suggests economic motivations are intertwined with military posturing. The IDF's continued operations in southern Lebanon despite the announced ceasefire, and its stated commitment to operating south of the yellow line, indicates a deliberate interpretation of the ceasefire terms that permits ongoing security activity. The identification of specific Israeli casualties from Battalion 52 and the Commando Brigade suggests ground operations are occurring in southern Lebanon.
